implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: Store factory async context manager with AsyncPostgresStore
Unit tests with no database/network dependencies
No direct driver imports in unit tier

completion_promises.criterion_text: `src/fleet_memory/store.py` exports `async_store_context(settings, embed_fn=None)` as an asynccontextmanager yielding `AsyncPostgresStore`; entering it runs `store.setup()`; exiting closes the pool cleanly
completion_promises.evidence: Created src/fleet_memory/store.py with async_store_context function decorated with @asynccontextmanager. Entry calls await store.setup() to initialize schema. Exit delegates to AsyncPostgresStore context manager protocol for pool closure. Index config includes {dims, embed, fields} matching langgraph-checkpoint-postgres >=2.0 signature.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `validate_namespace(("fleet_memory", "my-project", "chunk"))` raises `NamespaceValidationError` whose message states identifiers must use underscores; `validate_namespace(("fleet_memory", "fleet_memory", "adr"))` passes — enforcement happens before any database call
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented validate_namespace() using regex pattern ^[a-z0-9_]+$ to enforce underscores-only identifiers. Rejects hyphens, uppercase, dots, slashes, empty strings. Added NamespaceValidationError to errors.py with namespace and invalid_parts attributes. Error message explicitly states 'must use underscores only'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_store_validation.py -v` passes with no database and no network (the factory is constructed but never entered in the unit tier)
completion_promises.evidence: All 21 tests pass in 0.22s with no database or network dependencies. Tests construct the factory context manager but never call __aenter__ (unit tier hermeticity). Includes parametrized table with 15 namespace validation cases, explicit hyphen/valid tests, factory construction tests with/without embed_fn, and 2 seam tests.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `grep -rE "import psycopg|from psycopg|import asyncpg" tests/unit/` exits non-zero (no direct driver imports in unit tests — hermeticity of the unit tier)
completion_promises.evidence: Verified grep exits with code 1 (no matches found in test files). Only meta-references in test_settings.py checking that settings.py doesn't import psycopg. No direct psycopg or asyncpg imports in any unit test files, maintaining unit tier hermeticity.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Driver/API verification recorded (review risk R5): confirm against the installed `langgraph-checkpoint-postgres` version that (a) the conninfo is plain `postgresql://` psycopg3 format, (b) the index-config key shape `{dims, embed, fields}` matches the constructor signature, (c) pool min/max from settings flow into the actual pool — record findings as comments in `store.py`
completion_promises.evidence: Added comprehensive implementation notes in store.py documenting: (a) 'Driver: psycopg3 with psycopg-pool (plain postgresql:// conninfo, no +asyncpg)', (b) 'Verified contract: {dims: int, embed: callable, fields: list[str]}', (c) 'Pool sizing: from settings.pg_pool_min and settings.pg_pool_max' flowing through pool_kwargs in from_conn_string call. Verified against langgraph-checkpoint-postgres >=2.0.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
